meaning_english: Sense / Beauty. Example: Aesthetic (relating to beauty), Anaesthesia (loss of sensation).
Male / Man. Example: Android (robot resembling man), Misandry (hatred of men).
Book. Example: Bibliophile (book lover), Bibliography (list of books used in a report).
Bad / Harsh. Example: Cacophony (harsh sound), Cacography (bad handwriting).
People. Example: Pandemic (spread among all people), Demagogue (leader who appeals to people's emotions).
Power / Energy. Example: Dynamic (full of energy), Dynasty (powerful line of rulers).
Good / Well / Pleasant. Example: Euphoria (intense happiness), Euphemism (using a mild word for a harsh one).
Woman / Female. Example: Gynaecology (study of female health), Misogyny (hatred of women).
Sun. Example: Heliocentric (sun as center), Heliotherapy (sunlight therapy).
Peculiar / Own / Distinct. Example: Idiosyncrasy (peculiar habit), Idiom (distinct expression).
Inflammation / Swelling. Example: Arthritis (joint swelling), Gastritis (stomach inflammation).
Thousand. Example: Kilogram (1000 grams), Kilometer (1000 meters).
Fat. Example: Liposuction (removing fat), Lipids (fats in the body).
Word / Thought / Speech. Example: Prologue (words before), Logic (reasoning).
Large / Long. Example: Macroeconomics (study of large systems), Macromolecule (large molecule).
Great / Million / Large. Example: Megaphone (makes voice loud), Megacity (huge city).
New / Recent. Example: Neophyte (beginner/new convert), Neolithic (new stone age).
Straight / Correct. Example: Orthodontist (straightens teeth), Orthodox (correct/standard belief).
All / Any / Every. Example: Panacea (cure for all), Pan-India (across all India).
Sound. Example: Phonetics (study of sounds), Symphony (sounds together).
Many / Much. Example: Polyglot (speaks many languages), Polygamy (many marriages).
First / Original. Example: Prototype (first model), Protocol (first draft/rules).
False / Fake. Example: Pseudonym (false name), Pseudoscience (false science).
Mind / Soul. Example: Psychology (study of mind), Psychopath (mental illness).
Fire / Heat. Example: Pyrotechnics (fireworks), Pyretic (causing fever).
Far / Distant. Example: Telepathy (feeling from far), Telescope (seeing from far).
Beyond / Extreme. Example: Ultraviolet (beyond violet), Ultrasonic (beyond sound).
Foreign / Stranger. Example: Xenophobia (fear of foreigners), Xenon (a strange gas).
Animal. Example: Zoology (study of animals), Zodiac (circle of animal signs).
Form / Shape. Example: Amorphous (shapeless), Metamorphosis (change in form).
